target_game.name: Solitaire - Voodoo Card Game!
target_game.core_loop: Draw cards, stack them in descending alternating colors, and move them to foundation piles before the timer runs out.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual players of all ages, leaning towards adults seeking quick, familiar mental puzzles.
target_game.visual_style: Classic 2D flat design with a traditional green felt aesthetic.

palette.description: Traditional casino green background with stark white cards and bright red accents.

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: A suburban crime scene at night with police and forensic investigators.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Police tape being pulled across a mailbox and forensic photographers flashing cameras inside a house.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 5d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A suburban crime scene at night with police and forensic investigators."

top_archetypes.label: Fail · 3D-render

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 381d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A cat jumps on a keyboard, causing a man to fall backward in his chair."

centroid_hook.text_overlay: POV: When I got home, I opened Tetris Block Party.

top_archetypes.label: Fail · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: A player fails to move a blocked bus in a color-matching puzzle.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand icon taps a purple bus successfully, then taps a blocked blue bus causing a collision and a 'fail' sound effect.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: WHY THIS LEVEL IS SO HARD, CAN YOU WIN?

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 259d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A player fails to move a blocked bus in a color-matching puzzle."

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Cross-section reveal of an elaborate underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower.
centroid_hook.visual_action: The camera pans down from the Eiffel Tower to reveal a multi-story subterranean base with living quarters and labs.

top_archetypes.rationale: 4 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 216d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Cross-section reveal of an elaborate underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower"

game_fit_scores.rationale: Heavy frictions across the board: a dark 3D crime-scene render (navy/gold/brown palette, photoreal forensics) has zero visual or thematic continuity with a flat 2D green-felt solitaire game, and the hook telegraphs a narrative/mystery game (Merge Mansion, June's Journey) not a card sorter. Mechanic bridge is nearly impossible — there's no plausible 2-second pivot from "police tape on a mailbox" into draw/stack/foundation gameplay without feeling bait-and-switch, which risks store-policy flags and tanks post-install retention. Only weak positive is loose audience overlap (adult casual players enjoy mystery tropes); do not greenlight this archetype for Voodoo Solitaire — if curiosity is the desired pitch, commission a card-native mystery hook (e.g., "solve the case by clearing the tableau") instead.

This is a Gardenscapes-style 3D fail-skit hook with no card, stacking, or sorting cue — it has zero mechanical bridge to a classic solitaire core loop and would mislead-install at best. Visually it clashes hard: blue/green/white character-driven 3D render vs. the game's flat 2D casino-green, character-free, card-forward DNA. Casual adult overlap exists in theory, but without a believable solitaire payoff in the back half of the ad, IPM and retention will collapse — recommend skipping unless reworked into a "save the cat / solve the board" solitaire fail-puzzle variant.

Hard pass as-is: the hook is a live-action POV skit explicitly name-dropping a Tetris-style block puzzle, which clashes with Solitaire's core stacking/sorting loop and the classic green-felt 2D aesthetic — neither the purple/gold/cyan palette nor the youthful backflip energy aligns with the adult casual card audience. The archetype also has razor-thin support (n=1, single advertiser), so freshness/velocity signals are unreliable. Only worth testing if reshot for Solitaire with an age-appropriate actor, swapped overlay ("opened Solitaire"), and a gameplay payoff showing card foundation builds — otherwise skip.

This is a classic Bus Jam / parking-puzzle fail hook — visually and mechanically it has nothing to do with Solitaire's green felt + playing cards aesthetic, and the "blocked vehicle" fail beat doesn't translate to drawing/stacking cards. Casual puzzle audiences do overlap somewhat, but adapting this archetype would require a full creative rebuild (e.g., a "wrong card move blocks foundation" fail), at which point it's no longer this cluster. Recommend skipping unless the team specifically wants to test a hybrid fail-hook variant authored from scratch around card-stacking dead-ends.

This archetype is a near-total mismatch for a classic Solitaire title: the cross-section bunker reveal is a narrative/strategy-game trope (think Whiteout Survival, State of Survival) with dark earth-tone palettes that clash hard with the bright green-felt, card-forward visual language players expect from solitaire ads. There is no plausible bridge to drawing/stacking/sorting mechanics, and forcing one would produce a misleading "fake ad" that risks low IPM and high churn. Skip — or only consider if the team wants to test deceptive curiosity hooks, which is off-brand for a casual mental-puzzle audience.

brief.title: POV: Lunch Break Solitaire Run
brief.hook_3s: POV: 12 minutes left on lunch — phone out, thumb hovers over a red King on green felt, timer ticking.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Hands-only POV at an office desk slides phone into frame; screen lights up to the classic green-felt Solitaire tableau with the timer already counting down.
3-7s: Quick thumb taps drag a red Queen onto a black King, then cascade three alternating-color stacks in rapid succession — satisfying card-snap SFX.
7-11s: Foundation piles fill Ace→King with crisp flip animations; score counter spikes, undo button glows but goes unused.
11-14s: Final card auto-completes with confetti burst over the green felt; timer freezes with seconds to spare.
14-15s: App icon punches in over the felt with CTA.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into the casino green-felt heritage (#1A874B) with stark white cards (#FFFFFF) and bright red suit accents (#E31C1B). Classic 2D flat design — no 3D, no gradients beyond the felt texture. Vertical 9:16 phone-in-hand POV framing keeps the gameplay UI dominant on screen: visible timer, score counter, undo and finish buttons exactly as in the live build. Tense/challenging mood conveyed via ticking-clock SFX and quick-cut thumb drags rather than flashy VFX, preserving the adult-casual audience trust signal.
brief.rationale: Adapts the high-velocity "POV rush home" curiosity hook to a relatable adult micro-moment (lunch break) that matches Solitaire's casual-adult audience while preserving the archetype's first-person urgency. Pure in-game footage in the original green-felt palette protects fidelity and avoids the off-brand Tetris/backflip energy flagged in the fit scan. Recommend testing against a control of straight gameplay; expect lift on IPM from the curiosity overlay and tight 15s arc.

brief.title: One Move From Disaster — Solitaire Voodoo Fail
brief.hook_3s: Timer screams red. Three seconds left. One wrong card drag and the whole green-felt board collapses — can YOU save it?
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Tight zoom on a classic solitaire board, green felt, white cards mid-cascade. The timer flashes red 0:03 — a finger drags the WRONG card onto the wrong pile. A giant red 'FAIL ✗' stamp slams the screen.

3-7s TENSION: Camera pulls back to show the full tableau — foundations almost complete, only 2 red Aces missing. Voiceover-style overlay: 'Wait... it's still solvable.' A ghost-hand hesitates between two cards.

7-12s PAYOFF: Player POV nails the correct sequence — drag red Queen onto black King, flip hidden card (red Ace!), shoot it to the foundation. Score counter explodes upward, undo button glows, timer freezes at 0:01.
12-15s CTA: All four foundations complete with a satisfying card-stack snap. Green felt fills the frame, red 'YOU WIN' burst, app icon slides in with CTA.
brief.visual_direction: Stay 100% inside the game's DNA: flat 2D classic solitaire on traditional casino-green felt (#1A874B), crisp white playing cards (#FFFFFF) with sharp red suits and accents (#E31C1B). No 3D characters, no humans, no pets — the drama comes entirely from the cards, the timer, and the drag gesture. Use punchy zoom-ins on card flips, a slight screen-shake on the FAIL stamp, and a clean satisfying snap-to-foundation animation on the win. UI chrome (timer top-center, score counter, undo + finish buttons bottom) must match the actual app screenshots exactly.
brief.rationale: The Fail/3D-render archetype wins on pattern-interrupt + 'I can do better' bait, but the original cat-skit has zero mechanical bridge to solitaire and would mis-install. We port only the emotional beat (FAIL → recovery → win) and execute it with native solitaire gameplay so IPM and D1 retention hold. UA team: lean this on lookalikes of casual card/puzzle adults; expect strong CTR from the FAIL stamp and high IPM because the hook teaches the core loop on-screen.

brief.title: Blocked Foundation: The Solitaire Fail Hook
brief.hook_3s: A red Queen of Hearts hovers over a blocked foundation pile — timer ticking, the wrong move slams down, "FAIL" flashes across the green felt.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Tight zoom on a Solitaire tableau on green felt. A finger drags a red Queen toward a black King — overlay: 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?'
3-7s: Finger fumbles — drops the Queen on the wrong foundation pile. Cards shake red, buzzer sound, timer drops to 0:08. Overlay: 'WRONG MOVE!'
7-11s: Camera pulls back showing a fully blocked board, undo button pulsing red. Overlay: 'CAN YOU WIN?'
11-14s: Fast cut — a smarter sequence: alternating-color stack cascades down, foundations fill Ace→King in a satisfying combo. Overlay: 'YOUR TURN'
14-15s: End card with logo + CTA over green felt.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to the game's authentic look: deep casino green felt (#1A874B) as the table, crisp white cards (#FFFFFF) with bold red suits and red accent UI (#E31C1B) for the timer, fail flash, and undo button. Classic 2D flat design — no 3D, no characters, no mascots. Camera stays close to the tableau like a real gameplay capture, with a single human finger as the only "actor." Fail beat uses a red screen-shake + red vignette pulse to sell tension without breaking the flat aesthetic.
text_overlays: WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?
brief.rationale: Adapts the proven "fail-in-game" hook to Solitaire by reframing the blocked-bus beat as a blocked-foundation dead-end — a frustration every Solitaire player recognizes. UA team should A/B the "WRONG MOVE!" fail flash vs. a no-fail control; expect strong IPM lift from puzzle-curious adults who can't resist correcting a visible mistake. Keep all assets native to the game's green-felt UI to maximize click-to-install fidelity.
